intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe lack of biological material across all observed containers. The registry provided (P1-P4) does not match the current physical state of the desk.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.status: All registered specimens are currently absent or non-viable. The pots appear to contain only soil and debris.
plant_audit.observations: The containers are essentially empty of living botanical specimens. The 'white rabbit' anchor is the only consistent non-soil element.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely crushed eggshells or mineral deposits) on the surface of the black pot containing the rabbit anchor.
biome_observations.anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the yellow pot (T-4) and a cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) in the yellow pot (T-5, T-1) indicates human intervention/maintenance attempts.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_5: No significant change; pots appear empty.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Introduction of a spoon into the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2: Extreme low-light conditions; no visible plant growth.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Removal of the spoon; presence of a sensor/marker in the yellow pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. There is no evidence of active photosynthesis or plant life.
anomalies: Introduction of a sensor/marker stake (T-5, T-1).
Persistent presence of white granular debris in the P3 pot.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a consistent absence of the expected botanical specimens. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed propagation setup. The only notable changes over the 5-day period are the temporary placement of a spoon and a sensor stake, suggesting an attempt to monitor or amend the soil, though no plant life has emerged or survived to be documented.
